title: Mandarin in Minutes — Lesson 4: Questions
status: DRAFT — PRE-GATE. Gate 0 required before firing. Mandarin segments flagged for cousin review.
notes: This episode teaches question structure — the 'how to navigate' lesson.
Cousin review: all Mandarin dialogue lines flagged.
Key concept: ma-question particle and negation with bù.

config.voice_suffix: warm clear patient teacher voice, natural pacing, Mandarin demo segments delivered slowly and clearly, bilingual fluency implied

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @ZhangYutongLang-001 + @SET-LANG-BRIGHT-001 STYLE LOCK @STYLE-LANG-TUTOR-001: identical host face, sage green blouse, bright classroom backdrop. Zhang Yutong welcoming, confident, inviting — this lesson unlocks real navigation.
shots.speech_text: Lesson Four — questions. This is the lesson that gets you unstuck when you are lost, confused, or just curious. You are going to learn how to ask almost anything in Mandarin.

shots.cousin_review_note: Verify 'ma' particle explanation. 'Nǐ hǎo ma?' as 'How are you?' is standard. Verify 'Hǎo, xièxiè, nǐ ne?' response is natural.
shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @ZhangYutongLang-001 + @SET-LANG-BRIGHT-001 STYLE LOCK @STYLE-LANG-TUTOR-001: identical host face, sage green blouse — seamless continuation. Zhang Yutong demonstrating the ma-particle mechanic clearly, slight gesture pointing to end of phrase, light 'it is that simple' energy.
shots.speech_text: The simplest way to ask a yes-no question in Mandarin: add ma at the end. Nǐ hǎo — I know you know that. Nǐ hǎo ma? — How are you? Same phrase, one word added. The response: Hǎo, xièxiè, nǐ ne? — Good, thank you, and you?
shots.on_screen:
你好 → 你好吗？
Nǐ hǎo → Nǐ hǎo ma?
Hello → How are you?

好，谢谢，你呢？

shots.cousin_review_note: Verify 'Nǎlǐ/nǎr' usage (regional variation — nǎr is northern, nǎlǐ is more standard Mandarin). Both are acceptable. Verify 'Xǐshǒujiān zài nǎlǐ?' and 'Chūkǒu zài nǎr?' are natural.
shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @ZhangYutongLang-001 + @SET-LANG-BRIGHT-001 STYLE LOCK @STYLE-LANG-TUTOR-001: identical host face, sage green blouse — seamless continuation. Zhang Yutong illustrating navigation scenario — slight looking-around gesture, then warmly direct explanation.
shots.speech_text: To ask where: use nǎlǐ — where. Xǐshǒujiān zài nǎlǐ? — Where is the bathroom? Chūkǒu zài nǎr? — Where is the exit? Zài means is or located at. That word does a lot of work.
shots.on_screen:
哪里 nǎlǐ = where
在 zài = located at

洗手间在哪里？
Where is the bathroom?

出口在哪儿？
Where is the exit?

shots.cousin_review_note: Verify 'Zhè ge jiào shénme?' and 'Nà ge shì shénme?' are natural. Verify 'shénme' tones.
shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @ZhangYutongLang-001 + @SET-LANG-BRIGHT-001 STYLE LOCK @STYLE-LANG-TUTOR-001: identical host face, sage green blouse — seamless continuation. Zhang Yutong pointing gesture — demonstrating asking about objects, warm curious energy.
shots.speech_text: To ask what something is called: Zhè ge jiào shénme? — What is this called? Or Nà ge shì shénme? — What is that? Shénme means what. Jiào means called. Shì means is.
shots.on_screen:
什么 shénme = what

这个叫什么？
Zhè ge jiào shénme?
What is this called?

那个是什么？What is that?

shots.cousin_review_note: Verify 'bù' negation usage. 'Bù shì' (not is), 'Bù hǎo' (not good), 'Wǒ bù zhīdào' (I don't know) are all standard. Note: bù changes tone before another 4th-tone word (bú shì) — decide whether to mention this detail or keep simple.
shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @ZhangYutongLang-001 + @SET-LANG-BRIGHT-001 STYLE LOCK @STYLE-LANG-TUTOR-001: identical host face, sage green blouse — seamless continuation. Zhang Yutong with slightly amused friendly energy — the 'not' word is very useful and she knows it.
shots.speech_text: You need to say no, not, and I do not know. Bù is your negation. Bù shì — is not. Bù hǎo — not good. And the most honest phrase for a beginner: Wǒ bù zhīdào — I do not know. That will save you many times.
shots.on_screen:
不 bù = not / no

不是 bù shì = is not
不好 bù hǎo = not good

我不知道
Wǒ bù zhīdào
I don't know

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @ZhangYutongLang-001 + @SET-LANG-BRIGHT-001 STYLE LOCK @STYLE-LANG-TUTOR-001: identical host face, sage green blouse — seamless continuation. Zhang Yutong in practice mode, warm and encouraging, brief pauses after each prompt.
shots.speech_text: Practice. Ask me how I am: nǐ hǎo ma? Ask where the exit is: chūkǒu zài nǎlǐ? Tell me you do not know: wǒ bù zhīdào. Your study guide has these and more.
shots.on_screen:
你好吗？
出口在哪里？
我不知道

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @ZhangYutongLang-001 + @SET-LANG-BRIGHT-001 STYLE LOCK @STYLE-LANG-TUTOR-001: identical host face, sage green blouse — seamless continuation. Zhang Yutong warm, anticipatory sign-off — setting up the finale of the series.
shots.speech_text: Lesson Four done. You can ask where things are, what things are called, and say no to anything. Lesson Five is your first full conversation. Everything comes together. Zàijiàn.
